meaning: make an effort to / try to (habitual)

exercises.translation: I make an effort to exercise every day.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided to'; a firm decision, not ongoing effort.
つもりです means 'I intend to'; states intention, not habitual effort.
ことがあります means 'sometimes happens'; frequency, not deliberate effort.

exercises.example_sentence: 野菜をたくさん食べる＿＿。
exercises.translation: I try to eat lots of vegetables.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided to'; a one-time decision.
つもりです means 'I intend to'; intention, not ongoing effort.
ことがあります means 'it sometimes happens'; not deliberate effort.

exercises.translation: I make an effort not to be late.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています expresses a decision, not ongoing conscious effort.
つもりです states an intention, not habitual practice.
ことがあります means 'it sometimes happens'; about frequency.

exercises.translation: I try to go to bed early.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided to'; a firm decision.
つもりです means 'I plan to'; a stated intention.
たいです means 'I want to'; desire, not habitual effort.

exercises.translation: I make an effort to drink water every morning.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided to'; decision, not continuous effort.
つもりです means 'I intend to'; intention only.
ことがあります means 'it sometimes happens'; not deliberate.

exercises.translation: I try not to eat sweets.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided not to'; a one-time decision.
つもりです means 'I intend not to'; intention, not effort.
ことがあります means 'it sometimes happens'; about frequency.

exercises.translation: I make an effort to speak in Japanese.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided to'; a decision, not ongoing effort.
つもりです means 'I plan to'; intention, not habitual practice.
たいです means 'I want to'; desire, not practice.

exercises.translation: I try not to drink too much alcohol.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided not to'; a firm decision.
つもりです means 'I intend not to'; intention only.
ことがあります means 'it sometimes happens'; about frequency.

exercises.translation: I make an effort to practice kanji every day.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided to'; one-time decision.
つもりです means 'I plan to'; stated intention.
たいです means 'I want to'; desire, not effort.

exercises.example_sentence: エレベーターを使わない＿＿。
exercises.translation: I try not to use the elevator.
wrong_choice_explanations: ことにしています means 'I've decided not to'; decision, not ongoing effort.
つもりです means 'I don't intend to'; intention only.
ことがあります means 'it sometimes happens'; frequency, not effort.
